more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Pardoned inmates praise President
more.description: Source: Pardoned inmates praise President – herald Arron Nyamayaro-Herald Reporter MORE than 480 prisoners were released yesterday from Harare Central Prison after having sentences cut under the Presidential Clemency, expressing their heartfelt gratitude to President Mnangagwa for granting them an earlier second chance. The high walls of the prison reverberated with jubilant whistles and ululation [...]The post Pardoned inmates praise President appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation.

more.title: Heritage-based curriculum now compulsory
more.description: Source: Heritage-based curriculum now compulsory – herald Debra Matabvu-Senior Reporter ALL schools in Zimbabwe will be required to adopt the Heritage-Based Curriculum as their core syllabus from next year, with foreign curricula such as the Cambridge International Education pathway offered only as an optional add-on, subject to approval by the Ministry of Primary and Secondary [...]The post Heritage-based curriculum now compulsory appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation.

more.title: President sets 4 305 prisoners free
more.description: Source: President sets 4 305 prisoners free – herald Ivan Zhakata-Herald Correspondent President Mnangagwa has proclaimed Clemency Order No. 1 of 2026 granting amnesty to 4 305 inmates in a move aimed at promoting restorative justice and decongesting the country’s correctional facilities. Announcing the development, Justice, Legal and Parliamentary Affairs Minister Ziyambi Ziyambi said the President [...]The post President sets 4 305 prisoners free appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation.

more.title: Govt reaffirms raw mineral export ban
more.description: Source: Govt reaffirms raw mineral export ban – herald Wallace Ruzvidzo-Herald Reporter GOVERNMENT has reaffirmed its ban on the export of all raw minerals, clarifying that this restriction applies all minerals, not just lithium concentrates. In addition, the Government highlighted that the suspension, which came into effect last week, was indefinite. The decision reflects President [...]The post Govt reaffirms raw mineral export ban appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation.

more.title: Local tobacco firm starts producing cigarettes under toll manufacturing
more.description: Source: Local tobacco firm starts producing cigarettes under toll manufacturing – herald Martin Kadzere LOCAL tobacco company, Ethical Holdings, will start producing cigarettes under a tolling arrangement with a Chinese firm, in a development that will foster beneficiation and optimise export earnings from the golden leaf, an official has said. The move aligns with the [...]The post Local tobacco firm starts producing cigarettes under toll manufacturing appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation.

more.title: Companies fail to claim job incentives for employing youth
more.description: Source: Companies fail to claim job incentives for employing youth – herald Nqobile Bhebhe Zimpapers Business Desk FINANCE, Economic Development and Investment Promotion Minister Professor Mthuli Ncube has said no company has claimed the Government’s employment incentive schemes designed to stimulate job creation and uplift vulnerable groups. Prof Ncube, who has been vocal about the [...]The post Companies fail to claim job incentives for employing youth appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation.

more.title: ‘ZiG trust must be earned’
more.description: Source: ‘ZiG trust must be earned’ – herald Nelson Gahadza-Senior Business Reporter RESTORING confidence remains the cornerstone of the country’s Monetary Policy framework, while trust in the local currency cannot be legislated, but must be earned through sustained macro-economic stability and consistent policy implementation, the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe has said. Responding to questions from [...]The post ‘ZiG trust must be earned’ appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation.

more.title: Traffic Council to get arresting powers
more.description: Source: Traffic Council to get arresting powers – herald Freeman Razemba-Senior Reporter THE Traffic Safety Council of Zimbabwe is set to transform into an agency with arresting powers to improve its enforcement capabilities by June 2026, Transport and Infrastructural Development Minister Felix Mhona has said. Presently, the TSCZ is primarily responsible for raising road safety [...]The post Traffic Council to get arresting powers appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation.

more.title: Inmate jailed 15 years for attempting to evade justice
more.description: Source: Inmate jailed 15 years for attempting to evade justice – herald Zvikomborero Parafini-Court Reporter THE inmate who robbed a prison officer, stabbed him and wore his uniform in a brazen attempt to escape from the Harare Magistrates Court, has been sentenced to 15 years in prison. Itai Madhongo (30) was convicted after a full [...]The post Inmate jailed 15 years for attempting to evade justice appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation.

more.title: Gold mining certificate cancelled
more.description: Source: Gold mining certificate cancelled – herald Fidelis Munyoro-Chief Court Reporter A CERTIFICATE issued to MMP Mining Syndicate for gold mining on communal land in Zhombe is unlawful and must be cancelled, the High Court has ruled. Justice Evangelista Kabasa, sitting in Bulawayo, declared the certificate of registration issued on January 24, 2025, to first [...]The post Gold mining certificate cancelled appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation.

more.title: ZCTU pleads with Parliament, Rights Commission as torture cases over Constitutional Amendments escalate
more.description: THE Zimbabwe Congress of Trade Unions (ZCTU) has pleaded for the Zimbabwe Human Rights Commission (ZHRC) and Parliament’s intervention in the wake of rising cases of abductions and torture in recent weeks. Recent reports indicate a surge in political violence following the gazetting of Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3. The bill proposes extending presidential and legislative terms [...]The post ZCTU pleads with Parliament, Rights Commission as torture cases over Constitutional Amendments escalate appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com.
